title: మాట నిలబెట్టిన రాముడు — అడవి బాట
moral: పిల్లలూ, చూశారా, మన మాటను నిలబెట్టుకోవడం ఎంత గొప్పదో! కష్టం అనిపించినా, ధర్మం వైపు నడిస్తే మనసుకు ఎంత శాంతి, గౌరవం దొరుకుతుందో కదా.
main_character: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Always the same character across every scene.
setting: The ancient city of Ayodhya, a bustling capital filled with grand palaces, vibrant markets, and sacred temples. The story transitions to the serene, green forests of Dandakaranya. The time is during the day, moving from bright morning to contemplative afternoon.

scenes.text: అయోధ్య నగరం పండుగ శోభతో కళకళలాడుతోంది, నలుదిక్కులా మల్లెపూల దండల సువాసనలు గుప్పుమంటున్నాయి. రాజభవనంలో దీపాలు వెలుగుతున్నాయి, మంగళ వాయిద్యాలు మ్రోగుతున్నాయి — ప్రజల ఆనందం ఉప్పొంగుతోంది. రాజు దశరథుడు తన ప్రియకుమారుడైన రాముడిని చూసి గర్వంగా నవ్వాడు. "నా కుమారుడా, నీకు పట్టాభిషేకం చేయాలని ప్రజలు కోరుకుంటున్నారు," అని ఆయన మాటల్లో ప్రేమ తొణికిసలాడింది. రాముడు, తన కమలాల వంటి కళ్ళతో తండ్రిని చూస్తూ, వినయంగా తల వంచాడు, తన విధిని స్వీకరించడానికి సిద్ధంగా. అయితే, ఈ మహోత్సవం అనుకున్నట్లుగానే జరుగుతుందా...
scenes.image_prompt: The young man looks at his father and humbly bows his head. His lotus-like eyes reflect a readiness to accept his destiny. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: dasharatha — a well-known Hindu mythology figure, depicted accurately per canonical tradition. Bright, golden morning light streaming through palace arches, illuminating vibrant silks and decorations, full of joy and promise.
scenes.scene_visual: The young man looks at his father and humbly bows his head. His lotus-like eyes reflect a readiness to accept his destiny.
scenes.text_te_en: Ayodhya Nagaram Panduga Shobhato Kalakalalaadutondi, Naludikkulaa Mallepula Dandala Suvaasanalu Guppumantunnaayi. Raajabhavananlo Dipaalu Velugutunnaayi, Mangala Vaayidyaalu Mrogutunnaayi — Prajala Aanandam Uppongutondi. Raaju Dasharathudu Tana Priyakumaarudaina Raamudini Chusi Garvangaa Navvaadu. "Naa Kumaarudaa, Niku Pattaabhishekam Cheyaalani Prajalu Korukuntunnaaru," Ani Aayana Maatallo Prema Tonikisalaadindi. Raamudu, Tana Kamalaala Vanti Kallato Tandrini Chustu, Vinayangaa Tala Vanchaadu, Tana Vidhini Svikarinchadaaniki Siddhangaa. Ayite, I Mahotsavam Anukunnatlugaane Jarugutundaa...
scenes.text_en: The city of Ayodhya was sparkling with festive joy, and the sweet scent of jasmine garlands filled the air in every direction. Lights glowed in the royal palace, and auspicious music played — the people's happiness overflowed. King Dasharatha smiled proudly, looking at his beloved son, Rama. "My son, the people wish for you to be crowned," he said, his words full of love. Rama, looking at his father with his lotus-like eyes, humbly bowed his head, ready to accept his destiny. But, would this grand celebration happen as planned...?

scenes.text: కానీ ఆ ఆనందం ఎంతో సేపు నిలవలేదు — రాజభవనంలో ఒక్కసారిగా తీవ్రమైన నిశ్శబ్దం అలుముకుంది. కైకేయి రాణి తన రెండు కోరికలను వెల్లడించింది: రాముడు అడవికి వెళ్ళాలి, భరతుడు రాజు కావాలి. రాజు దశరథుడు ఆ మాటలు విని కూలబడిపోయాడు, అతని శరీరం వణకడం మొదలుపెట్టింది. అతని కళ్ళ నుండి నీళ్ళు ధారగా కారాయి, గుండె బరువెక్కినట్లు ఊపిరి పీల్చుకున్నాడు. రాముడు మాత్రం తన కమల నేత్రాలతో తండ్రిని చూస్తూ, ప్రశాంతంగా, "తండ్రీ, మీ మాట నా ఆజ్ఞ. నేను అడవికి వెళ్ళడానికి సిద్ధంగా ఉన్నాను," అని పలికాడు. ఈ ఊహించని, కఠినమైన ఆజ్ఞను రాముడు ఎలా స్వీకరించబోతున్నాడు?
scenes.image_prompt: The old king collapses to the ground, his body trembling uncontrollably. Tears stream from his eyes as he struggles to breathe, while the young man watches him calmly. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: dasharatha — a well-known Hindu mythology figure, depicted accurately per canonical tradition. Dim, heavy light in a private chamber, shadows clinging to corners, a sense of foreboding and profound despair.
scenes.scene_visual: The old king collapses to the ground, his body trembling uncontrollably. Tears stream from his eyes as he struggles to breathe, while the young man watches him calmly.
scenes.text_te_en: Kaani Aa Aanandam Ento Sepu Nilavaledu — Raajabhavananlo Okkasaarigaa Tivramaina Nishshabdam Alumukundi. Kaikeyi Raani Tana Rendu Korikalanu Velladinchindi: Raamudu Adaviki Vellaali, Bharatudu Raaju Kaavaali. Raaju Dasharathudu Aa Maatalu Vini Kulabadipoyaadu, Atani Shariram Vanakadam Modalupettindi. Atani Kalla Nundi Nillu Dhaaragaa Kaaraayi, Gunde Baruvekkinatlu Upiri Pilchukunnaadu. Raamudu Maatram Tana Kamala Netraalato Tandrini Chustu, Prashaantangaa, "Tandri, Mi Maata Naa Aajnya. Nenu Adaviki Velladaaniki Siddhangaa Unnaanu," Ani Palikaadu. I Uhinchani, Kathinamaina Aajnyanu Raamudu Elaa Svikarinchabotunnaadu?
scenes.text_en: But that joy didn't last long — a sudden, deep silence fell over the royal palace. Queen Kaikeyi revealed her two wishes: Rama must go to the forest, and Bharata must become king. King Dasharatha collapsed upon hearing those words, his body began to tremble. Tears streamed from his eyes, and he breathed heavily, as if his heart was burdened. Rama, however, looking at his father with his lotus eyes, calmly said, "Father, your word is my command. I am ready to go to the forest." How would Rama accept this unexpected, harsh command?

scenes.text: రాముడు తన ప్రియ సతి సీత వద్దకు వెళ్లి, అడవికి వెళ్ళవలసిన విషయాన్ని మెల్లగా వివరించాడు. అతని కంఠం కొద్దిగా వణికింది, సీత అడవి కష్టాలను ఎలా భరిస్తుందో అని అతని కనులలో ఆందోళన మెరిసింది. సీత ఆ మాటలు విని ఒక్క క్షణం నిశ్చలమై నిలబడింది, ఆమె నార వస్త్రం అంచు మెల్లగా కదిలింది — కానీ ఆమెలో ఎలాంటి బెరుకు లేదు. ఆమె కళ్ళు ధృడంగా చూస్తూ, "మీరు ఎక్కడికి వెళ్ళినా, నేను మీతోనే వస్తాను," అని స్పష్టంగా పలికింది. ఆమె మాటల్లో అచంచలమైన ప్రేమ, దృఢ సంకల్పం ప్రతిధ్వనించాయి. అడవి కష్టాలను తనతో పంచుకోవడానికి రాముడు సీతను అనుమతిస్తాడా?
scenes.image_prompt: The woman stands still, listening to the young man, then looks at him with firm resolve. The edge of her simple bark cloth (valkala) gently moves, but her eyes show no fear as she declares her intention. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Sita: a graceful young woman (mid-twenties) with a fair golden complexion and long dark hair adorned with simple jasmine flowers. Wearing simple saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala) — NOT silk saree, NOT gold ornaments. Eyes reflecting deep wisdom and quiet dignity. Serene, determined expression. Soft, contemplative light in a quiet inner courtyard, warm and intimate, reflecting a deep bond and shared destiny.
scenes.scene_visual: The woman stands still, listening to the young man, then looks at him with firm resolve. The edge of her silk saree gently moves, but her eyes show no fear as she declares her intention.
scenes.text_te_en: Raamudu Tana Priya Sati Sita Vaddaku Velli, Adaviki Vellavalasina Vishayaanni Mellagaa Vivarinchaadu. Atani Kantham Koddigaa Vanikindi, Sita Adavi Kashtaalanu Elaa Bharistundo Ani Atani Kanulalo Aandolana Merisindi. Sita Aa Maatalu Vini Okka Kshanam Nishchalamai Nilabadindi, Aame Pattuchira Anchu Mellagaa Kadilindi — Kaani Aamelo Elaanti Beruku Ledu. Aame Kallu Dhrridangaa Chustu, "Miru Ekkadiki Vellinaa, Nenu Mitone Vastaanu," Ani Spashtangaa Palikindi. Aame Maatallo Achanchalamaina Prema, Drridha Sankalpam Pratidhvaninchaayi. Adavi Kashtaalanu Tanato Panchukovadaaniki Raamudu Sitanu Anumatistaadaa?
scenes.text_en: Rama went to his beloved wife, Sita, and gently explained that he had to go to the forest. His voice trembled slightly, and worry flickered in his eyes about how Sita would endure the hardships of the forest. Sita stood still for a moment upon hearing those words, the edge of her simple bark cloth gently swayed — but there was no fear in her. Her eyes looked firm as she clearly said, "Wherever you go, I will come with you." Unwavering love and strong determination echoed in her words. Would Rama allow Sita to share the hardships of the forest with him?

scenes.text: ఈ వార్త విన్న లక్ష్మణుడు ఆగ్రహంతో ఊగిపోయాడు, అతని కళ్ళలో నిప్పులు చెరిగారు — వెంటనే రాముడి వద్దకు పరుగెత్తుకు వచ్చాడు. అతని భుజాలు ధృడంగా నిలబడ్డాయి, అతని మాటల్లో ఎలాంటి సందేహం లేదు. "అన్నా, నేను మీ సేవకుడిగా, మీ రక్షకుడిగా మీతో వస్తాను!" అని అతని గొంతులో దృఢ సంకల్పం ప్రతిధ్వనించింది. రాముడు, తన సోదరుడి నిస్వార్థ ప్రేమకు చలించిపోయాడు, అతని కళ్ళలో మెల్లగా నీరు నిలిచింది. లక్ష్మణుడు తన నిర్ణయంలో ఎలాంటి మార్పు లేదని తన ఆచరణతో స్పష్టం చేశాడు. ప్రియమైన భార్య, నమ్మకమైన సోదరుడు తన పక్కన నిలబడగా, రాముడు ఈ తెలియని అరణ్య ప్రయాణాన్ని ఎలా మొదలుపెడతాడు?
scenes.image_prompt: The young man's brother runs towards him, his shoulders firm, and declares his unwavering support. Fire sparks in the brother's eyes with rage, while the young man's eyes gently well up with tears. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Lakshmana: a young prince (mid-twenties) with a warm golden-brown complexion, muscular and alert — slightly shorter and more compact than Rama. During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot, carrying his bow and full quiver, always standing in a protective, watchful posture. He is NOT old, NOT a monk, NOT grey-haired — he is a vigorous young warrior-prince. Determined, loyal expression; the devoted younger brother. Strong, clear light in a training ground or open area, highlighting Lakshmana's firm stance, reflecting his unwavering resolve and strength.
scenes.scene_visual: The young man's brother runs towards him, his shoulders firm, and declares his unwavering support. Fire sparks in the brother's eyes with rage, while the young man's eyes gently well up with tears.
scenes.text_te_en: I Vaarta Vinna Lakshmanudu Aagrahanto Ugipoyaadu, Atani Kallalo Nippulu Cherigaaru — Ventane Raamudi Vaddaku Parugettuku Vachchaadu. Atani Bhujaalu Dhrridangaa Nilabaddaayi, Atani Maatallo Elaanti Sandeham Ledu. "Annaa, Nenu Mi Sevakudigaa, Mi Rakshakudigaa Mito Vastaanu!" Ani Atani Gontulo Drridha Sankalpam Pratidhvaninchindi. Raamudu, Tana Sodarudi Nisvaartha Premaku Chalinchipoyaadu, Atani Kallalo Mellagaa Niru Nilichindi. Lakshmanudu Tana Nirnayanlo Elaanti Maarpu Ledani Tana Aacharanato Spashtam Cheshaadu. Priyamaina Bhaarya, Nammakamaina Sodarudu Tana Pakkana Nilabadagaa, Raamudu I Teliyani Aranya Prayaanaanni Elaa Modalupedataadu?
scenes.text_en: Hearing this news, Lakshmana was filled with rage, and fire sparked in his eyes — he immediately ran to Rama. His shoulders stood firm, and there was no doubt in his words. "Brother, I will come with you as your servant, as your protector!" his voice echoed with strong resolve. Rama was deeply moved by his brother's selfless love, and tears gently welled up in his eyes. Lakshmana made it clear by his actions that his decision would not change. With his beloved wife and loyal brother standing by his side, how would Rama begin this unknown journey into the wilderness?

scenes.text: రాముడు, సీత, లక్ష్మణులు నార వస్త్రాలు ధరించి, రాజభవనం నుండి బయటకు అడుగు పెట్టారు. వారిని చూసిన అయోధ్య ప్రజల గుండెలు బద్దలయ్యాయి, వీధులన్నీ ఒక మూకుమ్మడి ఏడుపుతో నిండిపోయాయి — ఆ శబ్దం ఆకాశాన్ని తాకింది. రాముడు, తన ప్రశాంతమైన కమల నేత్రాలతో వారిని చూస్తూ, "నా తండ్రి ఆజ్ఞను నెరవేర్చడం నా ధర్మం," అని మెల్లగా పలికాడు. భరతుడు కూడా కన్నీళ్లతో తలవంచుకున్నాడు, తన అన్న పట్ల అపారమైన గౌరవం అతనిలో తొణికిసలాడింది. రాముడు ఒక్క మాట కూడా పలకకుండా ధర్మ మార్గాన్ని అనుసరించాడు. అయోధ్య ద్వారాలు దాటి బయట అడుగు పెడుతుండగా, ఆ విశాలమైన, తెలియని అడవి వారికి ఏం చూపించబోతోంది?
scenes.image_prompt: The young man, the woman, and the brother step out of the royal palace, dressed in simple bark clothes. They face a street filled with weeping people, while the young man's calm lotus-like eyes look upon the crowd. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Bharata: a young prince with a fair complexion, wearing a simple white dhoti and minimal ornaments (he lives in austerity while Rama is in exile). Gentle, grief-stricken yet dignified expression. Carries Rama's sandals (paduka) as a symbol of his brother's authority. Overcast, somber light on the outskirts of the city, long shadows casting a pall over the scene, reflecting the mood of departure and loss.
scenes.scene_visual: The young man, the woman, and the brother step out of the royal palace, dressed in simple bark clothes. They face a street filled with weeping people, while the young man's calm lotus-like eyes look upon the crowd.
scenes.text_te_en: Raamudu, Sita, Lakshmanulu Naara Vastraalu Dharinchi, Raajabhavanam Nundi Bayataku Adugu Pettaaru. Vaarini Chusina Ayodhya Prajala Gundelu Baddalayyaayi, Vidhulanni Oka Mukummadi Eduputo Nindipoyaayi — Aa Shabdam Aakaashaanni Taakindi. Raamudu, Tana Prashaantamaina Kamala Netraalato Vaarini Chustu, "Naa Tandri Aajnyanu Neraverchadam Naa Dharman," Ani Mellagaa Palikaadu. Bharatudu Kudaa Kannillato Talavanchukunnaadu, Tana Anna Patla Apaaramaina Gauravam Atanilo Tonikisalaadindi. Raamudu Okka Maata Kudaa Palakakundaa Dharma Maargaanni Anusarinchaadu. Ayodhya Dvaaraalu Daati Bayata Adugu Pedutundagaa, Aa Vishaalamaina, Teliyani Adavi Vaariki Em Chupinchabotondi?
scenes.text_en: Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana, dressed in bark clothes, stepped out of the royal palace. The hearts of Ayodhya's people broke upon seeing them, and the streets filled with a collective wail — that sound touched the sky. Rama, looking at them with his calm lotus eyes, gently said, "It is my duty (dharma) to fulfill my father's command." Bharata also bowed his head with tears, immense respect for his elder brother shone in him. Rama followed the path of dharma without uttering a single word. As they stepped out past the gates of Ayodhya, what would that vast, unknown forest show them?

scenes.text: రాముడు, సీత, లక్ష్మణులు అయోధ్యను వదిలి, సరయూ నది తీరానికి చేరుకున్నారు. ఒక చిన్న పడవలో కూర్చుని, ఆ పవిత్ర నదిని దాటడం ప్రారంభించారు — నీటి అలలు మెల్లగా పడవను తాకుతున్నాయి. రాముడు ప్రశాంతమైన చూపుతో అవతలి ఒడ్డును చూస్తూ, "ఇది కొత్త ప్రయాణానికి నాంది," అని ధృడంగా పలికాడు. సీత, లక్ష్మణులు కూడా అతని వైపు చూస్తూ, వారి నిశ్చయమైన మనసును ప్రదర్శించారు. వారి చూపుల్లో ఒక కొత్త జీవితానికి స్వాగతం పలికే సంకల్పం స్పష్టంగా కనిపించింది. రాముడు వెళ్ళిపోయాడు, మరి ఇప్పుడు అయోధ్యలో భరతుడు ఏం చేస్తాడు...
scenes.image_prompt: The young man, the woman, and the brother sit in a small boat, beginning to cross a sacred river. Water waves gently touch the boat as the young man looks towards the opposite bank with a calm, determined gaze. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Sita: a graceful young woman (mid-twenties) with a fair golden complexion and long dark hair adorned with simple jasmine flowers. Wearing simple saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala) — NOT silk saree, NOT gold ornaments. Eyes reflecting deep wisdom and quiet dignity. Serene, determined expression. Soft, late afternoon light on the river, golden hues reflecting on the water, a poignant beauty as they move forward into the unknown.
scenes.scene_visual: The young man, the woman, and the brother sit in a small boat, beginning to cross a sacred river. Water waves gently touch the boat as the young man looks towards the opposite bank with a calm, determined gaze.
scenes.text_te_en: Raamudu, Sita, Lakshmanulu Ayodhyanu Vadili, Sarayu Nadi Tiraaniki Cherukunnaaru. Oka Chinna Padavalo Kurchuni, Aa Pavitra Nadini Daatadam Praarambhinchaaru — Niti Alalu Mellagaa Padavanu Taakutunnaayi. Raamudu Prashaantamaina Chuputo Avatali Oddunu Chustu, "Idi Kotta Prayaanaaniki Naandi," Ani Dhrridangaa Palikaadu. Sita, Lakshmanulu Kudaa Atani Vaipu Chustu, Vaari Nishchayamaina Manasunu Pradarshinchaaru. Vaari Chupullo Oka Kotta Jivitaaniki Svaagatam Palike Sankalpam Spashtangaa Kanipinchindi. Raamudu Vellipoyaadu, Mari Ippudu Ayodhyalo Bharatudu Em Chestaadu...
scenes.text_en: Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana left Ayodhya and reached the banks of the Sarayu River. Sitting in a small boat, they began to cross the sacred river — water waves gently touched the boat. Rama, with a calm gaze, looked at the opposite bank and firmly said, "This is the beginning of a new journey." Sita and Lakshmana also looked at him, showing their determined minds. In their eyes, the resolve to welcome a new life was clearly visible. Rama had left, so what would Bharata do now in Ayodhya...?

scenes.text: అయోధ్యలో భరతుడు తన అన్నయ్య రాముడి పాదుకలను సింహాసనంపై పెట్టి, వాటిని రాజుగా గౌరవించాడు. "ఈ పాదుకలే అయోధ్యకు రాజు!" అని ప్రతీ ఒక్కరికీ స్పష్టం చేశాడు, అతని కళ్ళలో అపారమైన భక్తి, విధేయత మెరిశాయి. ఇటు అడవిలో రాముడు, సీత, లక్ష్మణులు ప్రశాంతంగా జీవిస్తున్నారు — నార వస్త్రాల స్పర్శ వారికి కొత్త అనుభూతినిచ్చింది. రాముడు ధర్మాన్ని నిలబెట్టినందుకు అతని ముఖంలో శాంతి, సంతృప్తి స్పష్టంగా కనిపించాయి. అతను తన తండ్రి మాటను నిలబెట్టి, ధర్మ మార్గాన్ని అనుసరించాడు, అందరికీ ఆదర్శంగా నిలిచాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The brother places a pair of sandals onto the royal throne, honoring them as king. Immense devotion and loyalty shine in his eyes as he makes this declaration to everyone present. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Bharata: a young prince with a fair complexion, wearing a simple white dhoti and minimal ornaments (he lives in austerity while Rama is in exile). Gentle, grief-stricken yet dignified expression. Carries Rama's sandals (paduka) as a symbol of his brother's authority. Two contrasting lights: a solemn, reverent glow in the Ayodhya throne room for Bharata, and a serene, dappled sunlight filtering through forest leaves where Rama walks, peaceful and natural.
scenes.scene_visual: The brother places a pair of sandals onto the royal throne, honoring them as king. Immense devotion and loyalty shine in his eyes as he makes this declaration to everyone present.
scenes.text_te_en: Ayodhyalo Bharatudu Tana Annayya Raamudi Paadukalanu Sinhaasanampai Petti, Vaatini Raajugaa Gauravinchaadu. "I Paadukale Ayodhyaku Raaju!" Ani Prati Okkariki Spashtam Cheshaadu, Atani Kallalo Apaaramaina Bhakti, Vidheyata Merishaayi. Itu Adavilo Raamudu, Sita, Lakshmanulu Prashaantangaa Jivistunnaaru — Naara Vastraala Sparsha Vaariki Kotta Anubhutinichchindi. Raamudu Dharmaanni Nilabettinanduku Atani Mukhanlo Shaanti, Santrripti Spashtangaa Kanipinchaayi. Atanu Tana Tandri Maatanu Nilabetti, Dharma Maargaanni Anusarinchaadu, Andariki Aadarshangaa Nilichaadu.
scenes.text_en: In Ayodhya, Bharata placed his elder brother Rama's sandals on the throne and honored them as king. "These sandals are the king of Ayodhya!" he made clear to everyone, and immense devotion and loyalty shone in his eyes. Meanwhile, in the forest, Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana were living peacefully — the touch of bark clothes gave them a new experience. Peace and contentment were clearly visible on Rama's face for upholding dharma. He kept his father's word, followed the path of righteousness, and became an ideal for everyone.

supporting_characters.description: Lakshmana: a young prince (mid-twenties) with a warm golden-brown complexion, muscular and alert — slightly shorter and more compact than Rama. During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot, carrying his bow and full quiver, always standing in a protective, watchful posture. He is NOT old, NOT a monk, NOT grey-haired — he is a vigorous young warrior-prince. Determined, loyal expression; the devoted younger brother. Always the same character across every scene.

Sita: a graceful young woman (mid-twenties) with a fair golden complexion and long dark hair adorned with simple jasmine flowers. During forest exile: wearing simple saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala) — NOT silk saree, NOT gold ornaments. Eyes reflecting deep wisdom and quiet dignity. Serene, determined expression. Always the same character across every scene.

Bharata: a young prince with a fair complexion, wearing a simple white dhoti and minimal ornaments (he lives in austerity while Rama is in exile). Gentle, grief-stricken yet dignified expression. Carries Rama's sandals (paduka) as a symbol of his brother's authority. Always the same character across every scene.
An elderly, regal king with a white beard and kind eyes, wearing rich royal garments. He carries the weight of his kingdom and his word. Always the same character across every scene.
A queen with a stern countenance, wearing elaborate royal attire in cool, deep colours. Her expression is often unyielding, driven by her desires. Always the same character across every scene.

quality_scores.notes: The story effectively conveys emotions and is grammatically flawless, making it a pleasant read. However, its primary weakness lies in its narrative arc and the explicit moralizing. To truly captivate a child and earn a higher score, the story must allow the moral to emerge organically from Rama's actions and struggles, rather than being stated directly by the narrator. Additionally, incorporating more concrete challenges and moments where Rama actively overcomes difficulties, rather than simply accepting them, would significantly enhance engagement and narrative tension.

moral_te_en: Pillalu, Chushaaraa, Mana Maatanu Nilabettukovadam Enta Goppado! Kashtam Anipinchinaa, Dharmam Vaipu Nadiste Manasuku Enta Shaanti, Gauravam Dorukutundo Kadaa.
title_en: Rama Upholds His Promise — The Forest Path
moral_en: Children, did you see how wonderful it is to keep our promises? Even when things are hard, following the path of what is right (dharma) brings so much peace and respect to our hearts, doesn't it?
